steps.title: Find the pivotal evidence
steps.content:
The finding states that “the prevalence of the practice described in the songs corresponded with growth in clam size and abundance despite increased harvesting pressure.” This is the crucial point: the songs accurately described a cultivation method that demonstrably worked.

Even under increased harvesting, clams thrived.

steps.title: Match the evidence to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice D correctly identifies what the finding proves: effective methods for cultivating food sources are among the knowledge preserved in Indigenous songs.

The other choices either overstate the scope (A claims this is the defining characteristic of all Indigenous music), introduce unsupported inferences (B speculates about Kwakwaka'wakw motivation; C invents a species difference with no basis), or miss what the evidence demonstrates.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
